operator_view: Batch 2 readiness remediation plan is structurally sound with five clear tracks addressing known Batch1 failures. The plan preserves governance invariants and fail-closed behavior per stated constraints. Before execution approval, five items need resolution: (1) explicit testable acceptance criteria for each track, (2) verification that automation maintains module boundaries, (3) enumerated state consistency rules, (4) confirmation governance paths are excluded from split logic, and (5) explicit rollback procedures. These are not blocking issues but require evidence of compliance before proceeding to build.

claims.statement: Remediation scope is well-defined across five remediation tracks: deterministic challenger validation, task-spec contract linting, steward diff preflight + split logic, attempt ledger continuity automation, and state/plan consistency cleanup. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:objective
Constraints explicitly preserve governance-protected paths, fail-closed behavior, and repo-clean/no-orphans invariants. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints
Production readiness criteria document (LifeOS_Build_Loop_Production_Plan_v2.1.md) provides authoritative acceptance criteria framework. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts
Batch1 failure modes documented in BurnIn report provide empirical basis for remediation prioritization. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts
State document (LIFEOS_STATE.md) provides current operational baseline for consistency verification. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts
Change class is 'amend' with moderate reversibility, indicating incremental rather than wholesale changes. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:header
Model plan uses independent challenger (glm-5) with primary (kimi-k2.5) and seat override for Architecture (minimax-m2.5), providing adequate review diversity. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:header

Acceptance criteria may not be sufficiently concrete/testable for all five remediation tracks. The constraint 'Approval requires concrete, testable acceptance criteria' is stated but evidence of these criteria in artifacts is not verified in this review. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence: Need to verify each remediation track has explicit testable success conditions in the production plan

Attempt ledger continuity automation may introduce new coupling between runtime_core and interfaces modules. The blast_radius is 'module' but automation could create cross-module dependencies. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence: Need to verify automation design maintains module boundaries

State/plan consistency cleanup scope is ambiguous - unclear if it covers all state drift scenarios or only known failure modes from Batch1. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence: Need explicit enumeration of state consistency rules to be enforced

Steward diff preflight + split logic - split logic could violate 'no modifications to governance protected paths' if governance validation is in the split path. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence: Need to verify split logic architecture does not touch governance paths

Medium uncertainty rating suggests execution risk exists; no explicit risk mitigation or rollback plan detailed in scope. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence: Need explicit rollback procedures for each remediation track

Add explicit testable acceptance criteria for each of the five remediation tracks, mapped to specific success/failure conditions. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints#'Approval requires concrete, testable acceptance criteria'
Verify attempt ledger continuity automation maintains module boundaries between runtime_core and interfaces; document any new cross-module contracts. [ASSUMPTION]

Enumerate specific state consistency rules to be enforced in cleanup, referencing known Batch1 drift scenarios from BurnIn report. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts#docs/11_admin/Batch1_BurnIn_Report.md

Confirm steward diff split logic does not traverse or modify governance-protected paths; document any path exclusions. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints#'No modifications to governance protected paths'
Add explicit rollback procedures for each remediation track to address medium uncertainty. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:header#uncertainty
